rationale: Codebase-pin lens: HEAD confirmed at 4dfebbcb, exactly the 9 files in scope changed, no internal/bead. All named symbols exist: Result.UnresolvedVerdicts (tally.go:162), leg 9.5 in gate.go (comment at :126, code at :258-272), Result.VoteDecision (tally.go:199) with the identical unresolved check. All four named tests exist and pass verbosely: TestPanelGateDecision_UnresolvedRequestChangesBlocks (3 subtests: RC-slot, unrecognized-verdict, two-RC), TestPanelGate_RequestChangesBlocksComplete, TestPanelGateDecision_ApprovesExcludesUnresolvedVerdicts, TestPanelGateDecision_ThresholdFloorIsLayeredNotReplaced. go build ./... clean, gofmt -l empty, go vet clean. go test ./internal/panel ./internal/complete ./cmd/mindspec all PASS (fresh, -count=1). internal/instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads fails in isolation as the known pre-existing z4ps flake — unrelated to this bead's files, not counted against it. Noted mid-review: the shared worktree briefly showed an uncommitted mutation to gate.go's leg-9.5 condition (likely a concurrent reviewer's mutation-probe), which I reverted; final state is clean at the pinned commit.

findings.description: Mid-review the shared bead worktree showed a transient uncommitted diff in internal/panel/gate.go (condition changed to 'false && len(unresolved) > 0'), consistent with a concurrent reviewer's mutation-probe running in the same worktree rather than a defect in the bead's commit. Reverted with `git checkout -- internal/panel/gate.go` to restore the pinned state; git status is clean and HEAD is 4dfebbcba61bd571d15b8c4788752409504d017d. Flagging as a process hazard for panel orchestration (shared worktree across parallel reviewers), not a code finding.
